requirements.summary: Apply a cohesive dark AI-tech visual theme with deep charcoal backgrounds and electric-green accents across the entire site
acceptanceCriteria: Global CSS/Tailwind config uses the dark background and electric-green accent throughout
All cards, buttons, and nav elements follow the same theme
No page breaks the established color and typography system

file_operations.description: Update global CSS to define the dark AI-tech theme: set near-black backgrounds (#0D0D0D, #141414), electric-green accent color (#00FF87), clean sans-serif typography, and smooth gradient highlights. Apply glassmorphism card styles with subtle transparency and backdrop blur.
Extend Tailwind theme with custom colors (charcoal backgrounds, electric-green accent) and typography settings to support the dark modern aesthetic across all components.

requirements.summary: Build a responsive landing page with hero, how-it-works, features, target audience, and footer sections
acceptanceCriteria: Hero section renders with headline, sub-headline, and CTA button
CTA scrolls to the upload tool section
'How It Works' section shows 3 numbered steps
Features section shows 6 feature cards
Footer renders with nav links and brand name
Page is fully responsive on mobile, tablet, and desktop

file_operations.description: Create the landing page component with: a hero section (headline 'Remove Backgrounds in One Click', sub-headline, CTA button 'Try It Free — Upload Now' that scrolls to #upload-tool), a 'How It Works' section (3 steps: Upload → AI Removes Background → Download with icons from frontend/public/assets/generated/step-upload.dim_128x128.png, step-ai.dim_128x128.png, step-download.dim_128x128.png), a Features section (6 feature cards: one-click removal, drag-and-drop, multiple formats, transparent PNG output, fast processing, secure handling), a Target Audience section, and a footer with brand name and nav links. Integrate the hero background image from frontend/public/assets/generated/hero-bg.dim_1920x1080.png. Ensure full responsiveness.

requirements.summary: Build a fully functional background removal tool with drag-and-drop upload, canvas-based processing, before/after preview, and PNG download
acceptanceCriteria: Drag-and-drop and click-to-browse both work for image upload
JPG, PNG, and WEBP files are accepted; invalid types show an error
Files over 10MB are rejected with an error message
An animated loader/spinner appears during processing
Before/after preview renders showing original and processed image
Download button triggers download of the result as a PNG file
The section is accessible from the landing page CTA

file_operations.description: Create the background removal tool component with: a drag-and-drop upload zone that also supports click-to-browse (accept JPG, PNG, WEBP; 10MB max size; show errors for invalid files), an animated processing state (spinner/loader), a side-by-side or toggle before/after preview of original and result images, a 'Remove Background' action button, and a 'Download PNG' button that triggers download. Implement canvas-based background removal by removing white/near-white pixels or applying a chroma-key effect (no external API). Call the backend recordProcessed method after successful processing.

Create a custom hook to handle the canvas-based background removal logic: load image onto canvas, iterate pixels to detect and remove white/near-white background, return processed image as PNG data URL. Include state management for upload, processing, and result.
Add an id='upload-tool' anchor section on the Home page and embed the BackgroundRemovalTool component there so the hero CTA can scroll to it.

requirements.summary: Build a Pricing page with three plan tiers (Free, Pro, Business) and pay-per-use note
acceptanceCriteria: Three plan cards render: Free, Pro, Business
Correct pricing and feature lists are displayed for each plan
Pro plan has a 'Most Popular' badge
Pay-per-use note is visible on the pricing page
CTA buttons are present on each card
Page is accessible from the main navigation

file_operations.description: Create the Pricing page with three plan cards: (1) Free (5 removals/day, standard quality, no card required), (2) Pro ($9.99/month, unlimited removals, HD quality, priority processing, 'Most Popular' badge), (3) Business ($29.99/month, unlimited, API access, bulk processing, dedicated support). Each card shows plan name, price, feature list, and CTA button. Include a pay-per-use note: 'Need more? Buy credits — $0.10 per image.'

requirements.summary: Add a persistent sticky top navigation bar with logo, nav links, and 'Get Started' CTA; collapse into hamburger menu on mobile
acceptanceCriteria: Navbar is visible and sticky on all pages
All nav links navigate to the correct sections/pages
'Get Started' button links to the upload tool
On mobile, nav collapses into a hamburger menu that opens/closes a drawer

file_operations.description: Create a sticky navbar component with: SnapCut AI logo/brand mark on the left (use frontend/public/assets/generated/logo-mark.dim_256x256.png), navigation links ('Home', 'Tool', 'Pricing', 'FAQ'), and a 'Get Started' CTA button on the right that links to #upload-tool. On mobile, collapse nav into a hamburger menu that opens/closes a drawer.
Add the Navbar component at the top of the app layout so it appears on all pages and remains sticky on scroll.

requirements.summary: Add a FAQ section/page with at least 6 expandable questions and answers
acceptanceCriteria: At least 6 FAQ items are displayed
Each item is an expandable accordion with question and answer
FAQ is accessible from the navigation

file_operations.description: Create the FAQ page with at least 6 expandable accordion items covering: supported formats, processing time, whether images are stored, what the free plan includes, how to upgrade, and data privacy/deletion policy.

requirements.summary: Display the live backend usage counter on the landing page hero as social proof
acceptanceCriteria: Frontend reads the counter via React Query and displays it on the hero section
Counter updates after each removal operation on the tool page

file_operations.description: Create a React Query hook to fetch the total processed count from the backend getTotalProcessed method. Use the backend interface method getTotalProcessed() which returns a Promise<bigint>.
Import and use the useProcessedCount hook in the hero section to display the live count as a social proof stat (e.g., 'X images processed'). Format the bigint value appropriately for display.

After successful background removal, call the backend recordProcessed method using the backend interface to increment the counter. Invalidate the React Query cache for the processed count so the hero stat updates.

requirements.summary: Wire generated static image assets (hero background, logo mark, step icons) into the frontend components
acceptanceCriteria: Hero background image renders in the hero section
Logo mark renders in the navbar and hero
Three step icons render in the 'How It Works' section
All images load from /assets/generated path

file_operations.description: Wire the hero background image (frontend/public/assets/generated/hero-bg.dim_1920x1080.png) into the hero section as a background image. Wire the three step icons (frontend/public/assets/generated/step-upload.dim_128x128.png, step-ai.dim_128x128.png, step-download.dim_128x128.png) into the 'How It Works' section for each respective step.
Wire the SnapCut AI logo mark (frontend/public/assets/generated/logo-mark.dim_256x256.png) into the navbar on the left side as the brand logo.
